topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — c6 verification of c5 major patches (TLDR + cross-refs)
change_summary: Decider c5 self-apply commit ef30b97 fixed two major c5 findings: (H1) TLDR factual error about pole location and the bogus 'single exponential' math claim, and (H2) two broken V6-era cross-references in detail_evidence. c6 verifies both fixes and carries forward the four still-open minor/moderate c5 findings that are already tracked as ISS-1243/1244/1245/1246.
our_argument_summary: WIN-053 is Self-Contradicted. The dome's 'Closed Toroidal Ovoid' claims a ring-magnet topology in which aetheric medium flows out of the Axis Mundi, across the disc, and back through a sub-terrestrial 'Sump.' In any such closed magnetic circuit, flux conservation forces source and return apertures to differ in field strength by the inverse of their area ratio. The dome's own geometry — a small polar aperture (area ~7.9e5 km^2 for a 500 km radius) vs the full-disc return (area ~1.26e9 km^2) — implies a ~1,600:1 asymmetry, predicting B_south ~39 nT. But the dome's fitted B(r) gives B_south = 64,852 nT: a 1,660:1 internal contradiction. The fitted coefficients A=62,376 and C=64,852 nT track observed WMM pole strengths (no derivation from dome geometry), and the exponential form is not derivable from a toroidal circuit. The c5 patches fixed a TLDR factual error (pole location, 'single exponential' claim) and two broken cross-references; c6 confirms those fixes are correct.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The WIN-053 claim is static prose on the predictions page. The scale length lambda = 8,619 km appears throughout the dome raw text as a KEY V50.6 RESULT in H(r) = 8537 * exp(-r/8619) km (verified in raw-text/05-model.txt line 33 and raw-text/01-home.txt line 28). The same 8,619 km is reused for B(r) without independent derivation. No dome-side script computes B(r) in this snapshot.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static prose only — no live monitoring, no comparison against observed magnetic data. The 'confirmation' is literary (claims 20% RMS globally) rather than produced by any automated pipeline.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Falsification threshold is stated as +/-15% deviation from lambda = 8,619 km in future fits — a window so loose that any future fit converging near this value cleanly 'passes,' and one outside it would still be ~1% of the 1,600:1 self-contradiction we already show.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None found. c5 already validated monitoring=none; c6 re-checks grep for 'WIN-053', '62,376', '64,852', 'ring magnet', 'toroidal' in raw-text/*.txt — none appear except lambda=8619 in H(r) contexts. Consistent with the detail_evidence claim 'monitor.py does not track WIN-053.'

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: grep on raw-text/*.txt for 'WIN-053', '62,376', '64,852', 'ring magnet', 'toroidal', 'Closed Toroidal' returns zero matches. Only references to 8619 are in H(r) contexts (firmament height), not B(r). The dome's raw text does not implement or monitor a B(r) prediction. Consistent with prior cycles.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Geomagnetic field structure — including dipolar asymmetry, polar field magnitudes, and the South Atlantic Anomaly — is predicted and measured with ~50 nT RMS globally by IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 / WMM using degree-13 spherical harmonics grounded in geodynamo physics. The dome's ~20% RMS (~10,000-13,000 nT error) is 200x worse. The dome renames the mechanism (aetheric toroidal flow) but does not predict new, testable numerical structure — and cannot reproduce localized anomalies or secular variation that the standard model handles routinely.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: A=62,376 nT and C=64,852 nT align with observed pole field magnitudes (NMP ~54-56k; SMP ~65-67k per WMM2025) published for decades before V51. The dome provides no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, lambda, or any dome parameter. Furthermore, the dome's own registration log shows PRED-TOROID-002 was filed 2026-04-04 while the claimed 'confirmation' date is 2026-03-23 — the confirmation predates registration, making this a retrodiction registered after the fact (detail_extra).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site derives A=62,376 or C=64,852 from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any other dome parameter. Only lambda=8,619 km is shared between H(r) and B(r), which is cross-domain parameter reuse rather than a derivation. The exponential form itself is also not derived from the claimed toroidal topology — a wound-solenoid toroid gives B proportional to 1/r, not e^(-r/lambda).

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence overstates the coefficient-to-observation match. Text: 'Both coefficients match the observed WMM pole strengths precisely because they were fitted to them.' Observation: A=62,376 nT vs ~54,000-56,000 nT at NMP is a ~11-15% gap; C=64,852 nT vs ~65,000-67,000 nT at SMP is within ~3%. The detail_evidence correctly uses the softer 'closely match,' but the TLDR escalates to 'precisely.' A careful defender will object that A does not 'precisely' match north pole observations — and the fit-amplitude is in any case not the at-pole field (the other exponential decays in by ~0.098 at r=20,015 km with lambda=8,619 km, adding another ~6,400 nT). Recommendation: change 'precisely because they were fitted' to 'closely because they were fitted' (one-word edit) OR add a half-sentence acknowledging that A and C are decay amplitudes rather than at-pole values. Keeps the core 'fitted, not derived' argument intact.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the word 'precisely' with 'closely' in tldr_evidence — preserves the argument without handing a defender an easy cavil. No detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text change needed.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference format inconsistency in the TLDRs vs the detail block. The tldr_verdict contains plain-text '(see §6.1)' while the c5 patch correctly upgraded detail_evidence's Section-6.4 reference to a clickable Section 6.4. For a reader who only reads the TLDR, '(see §6.1)' is non-navigable and they cannot find §6.1 without knowing it lives in the predictions tab. Section 6.1 is 'Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters' (verified at id="p6-61-0" inside the predictions tab div). Recommendation: upgrade to §6.1. Same UX pattern the c5 patch established for §6.4.

STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1243 (c5 H3 / c4 H2 / c3 Q3): sub-terrestrial return-channel defender rescue not explicitly preempted. The dome can argue C = 64,852 nT describes the re-entry aperture area above the Sump (not the south pole), which would be symmetric to the Axis Mundi. c6 does not re-raise this — ISS-1243 is already open and captures the required text additions. Confirming here only that the c5 patch did not incidentally preempt it.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; close when ISS-1243 is integrated.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1244 (c5 H6 / c4 H6): 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row not yet added. Current text has 500 km (1,660:1) and 1,000 km (415:1); a 3,000 km row would close any 'but the aperture is even bigger' wiggle room. c6 confirms the row is still missing and that ISS-1244 captures the fix.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; close when ISS-1244 is integrated.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1245 (c5 H7 / c4 H7): flux direction (source-vs-sink polarity) not discussed. The fitted B(r) sums two positive-signed exponentials — topologically a source-source pair, not a source-sink pair. A real ring magnet requires opposite polarity at source and return. c6 confirms this is still absent from the prose and that ISS-1245 captures the fix.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; close when ISS-1245 is integrated.

holes_found.description: STILL OPEN — tracked as ISS-1246 (c5 H8 / c4 H4): Sub-H(r) formula with delta = 6,371 km has no dome-site citation. detail_extra notes this is Earth's mean radius (a globe-derived constant) but does not link to where on the dome site the formula appears. c6 confirms the href is still missing.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; close when ISS-1246 is integrated.

stronger_arguments.description: ADVOCATE-MODE SOLENOID RETREAT. A dome defender's strongest escape is to drop the 'ring magnet' / closed-circuit framing and keep the two-term exponential as a purely phenomenological fit. That move concedes the central WIN-053 claim — 'the toroidal geometry predicts a dipolar field' — because without the circuit topology there is no geometric derivation, only a two-parameter fit to observation. Our detail_evidence already establishes this by noting that the exponential form is not derivable from a wound toroid. We could tighten this by adding one sentence in detail_verdict_text: 'Any defense that abandons the ring-magnet topology to keep the fit also abandons the WIN-053 claim — a two-parameter fit with no geometric derivation is exactly what §6.1 calls a hidden fitted parameter.'
sources: This review; the existing §6.1 'Zero Fitted Parameters' section.

stronger_arguments.description: CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY — THE LAMBDA = 8,619 km TRIPLE USE. lambda appears in H(r) (firmament height, WIN-034 family), B(r) (this WIN), and is also referenced in context of Schumann resonance and SAA patterns (per §6.1 prose). A single fitted parameter pressed into service for three structurally unrelated physical domains is the core structural argument against the 'zero fitted parameters' headline. The current WIN-053 detail_evidence mentions only the H(r)/B(r) pairing. Extending to name the broader set (even in one clause) would make the parameter-reuse critique harder to wave away. Already captured structurally in §6.1; a one-line echo in WIN-053 would reinforce the cross-link.
sources: sections.json part6 section id=p6-61-0 (Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters); detail_evidence for WIN-053, WIN-034.

stronger_arguments.description: QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION — AT-POLE FIELD, NOT AMPLITUDE. At the north pole (r_N=0, r_S=20015 km), B(N) = A + C * e^(-2.322) = 62376 + 64852*0.0982 ~= 68,744 nT, versus observed NMP ~55,000 nT. At the south pole, B(S) = A * e^(-2.322) + C = 62376*0.0982 + 64852 ~= 70,977 nT, versus observed SMP ~66,000 nT. These are 25% and 8% above observation respectively — consistent with the dome's stated 20% global RMS error and directly refuting any suggestion that A and C are 'the pole field values.' The current detail_evidence is close to this point but never computes B at the poles explicitly. Worth one sentence: 'At the literal poles the formula gives ~68,700 nT (N) and ~71,000 nT (S) — both ~25% and ~8% above observed, consistent with the 20% RMS the dome reports globally.' This also pre-empts any defender who says 'of course A and C match the observed fields at the poles.'
sources: Computed in c6 from published A, C, lambda, disc_radius.

kernel_of_truth.description: The V50.6 -> V51.0 architectural shift from a monopolar field to a two-pole structure reflects sound physical reasoning: a single-pole field cannot reproduce Earth's observed dipolar structure, and recognizing this is genuine insight. The functional form B(r) = A*exp(-r_N/lambda) + C*exp(-r_S/lambda) also has desirable properties — localized peaks at both poles, smooth interpolation, radial symmetry — that make it a reasonable empirical ansatz.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The instinct is right but the execution manufactures a new, quantitatively testable self-contradiction that V50.6's monopolar model did not have. A monopole has no return path to conserve, so flux conservation cannot be violated by construction. By adding a toroidal circuit in V51.0, the dome promised a ring-magnet topology whose conservation laws its own fitted formula violates by three orders of magnitude. Getting the phenomenology (dipolar-looking field) right while creating a new structural contradiction is not a refinement — it is a strictly worse model on its own terms. The 'right instinct, wrong execution' pattern also applies: a defender cannot recover the claim by appealing to physical intuition, because the specific topology the dome chose is precisely what generates the 1,600:1 contradiction.

deeper_analysis:
The c5 patches landed in commit ef30b97 and resolved both c5 major findings: (1) the TLDR factual error — 'same location (north)' is now correctly 'opposite ends of the disc,' and the incorrect 'simplifies to a single exponential' claim is removed. The dome's formula A*exp(-r_N/lambda) + C*exp(-r_S/lambda) is in fact a shifted-cosh-like function when A ~= C along a line from N to S (because r_N + r_S is roughly constant), which is NOT a single exponential. The decider's choice to drop the claim entirely rather than substitute the more accurate 'approximately a hyperbolic cosine' is defensible — the cosh observation is analytically true but not critical to the self-contradiction argument, which rests on flux conservation. (2) The two broken cross-references are now clickable anchors: Section 6.4 resolves to 'Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions' inside the predictions tab (verified: the
block exists and carries that heading), and WIN-012 resolves inside the wins tab (verified: id="win012" exists and the tab pattern matches four other WIN-012 links in the HTML). Remaining work is carried by the four open issues ISS-1243..1246 plus two minor items this review surfaces — both are polish rather than argument failure: (a) Word choice 'precisely' vs 'closely' in tldr_evidence. The detail_evidence uses 'closely match' (~13% off for A, ~3% for C). The TLDR's 'precisely' is strictly an overstatement and the kind of cavil a careful defender will chew on. One-word edit. (b) Cross-reference format inconsistency: tldr_verdict's '(see §6.1)' is plain-text while the detail_evidence now uses clickable anchors. This is a UX regression relative to the rest of the patched block — readers who only read the TLDR cannot navigate to §6.1. Upgrade to a clickable anchor. Advocate-mode stress test: the strongest defender move is to retreat from 'ring magnet topology' to 'phenomenological two-pole fit.' This concedes the central WIN-053 claim (the toroidal geometry is what is supposed to be predicting the dipolar field) and makes A, C, lambda all fitted parameters — directly contradicting the 'zero fitted parameters' headline the dome advertises. The detail_evidence captures this implicitly; a single explicit sentence would harden it. Cross-WIN consistency: lambda=8,619 km is used in H(r) (firmament height) and B(r) (magnetic field); §6.1 extends this to Schumann and SAA claims. The structural argument 'single fitted parameter pressed into service for unrelated domains' is already in §6.1; WIN-053 mentions the H(r)/B(r) pairing but could strengthen with a one-clause echo naming the broader set. Quantitative verification: computing B at the literal poles gives ~68,744 nT (N) and ~70,977 nT (S) versus observed ~55,000 / ~66,000 — a 25% / 8% overshoot. Consistent with the dome's stated 20% global RMS. This explicit calculation is a good pre-emption for any defender who might say 'of course A and C are the pole field values.' Verdict: Self-Contradicted remains correct. current_verdict_holds = true. recommended_action = minor_edit — both c5 major findings are resolved; remaining moderate/minor items are tracked as open issues plus two new minor polish items surfaced here.

Most likely Tarduno et al. 2019 'Antiquity of the South Atlantic Anomaly' or a sibling SAA-origins paper based on DOI prefix (Elsevier EPSL, 2018/12 issue). Contextually consistent with the SAA mention. c6 could not verify further in-sandbox; recommend confirming title/author on the next run that has outbound HTTP.
